claim_chain: CRZ is a novel endogenous female signaling component in post-mating response
CRZ is not transferred from males but acts as an endogenous peptide from female neuroendocrine cells
CRZ signaling decreases female receptivity and increases oviposition in mated females
CrzR is predominantly expressed in female reproductive tract, especially spermatheca and pouched gland
CRZ may regulate release of stored sperm and seminal fluid, gating male-derived factors
CRZ function is conserved in D. melanogaster, showing similar PMR modulation
CRZ and GnRH are paralogs sharing an ancient reproductive function

typical_pattern: Opening claim of novelty or main finding
Contrast with known mechanisms in other species

likely_priorities: Mechanistic clarity of CRZ signaling pathway from brain to reproductive tract
Direct evidence for hormonal versus local neuronal CRZ action
Identification of sensory inputs triggering CRZ release at mating
Demonstration of evolutionary conservation across insect orders
Clarification of interactions between CRZ and male-derived seminal factors
likely_attacks: Lack of identified inputs triggering CRZ release at mating
Inability to exclude local CRZ signaling in brain circuits versus hormonal action
Limited mechanistic detail on how CRZ regulates sperm/seminal fluid release
Incomplete characterization of CRZ function in post-mating metabolism
Need for direct demonstration of CRZ neuron activation upon copulation
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The discussion explicitly states outstanding questions: 'The inputs triggering release of hormonal CRZ at mating are not known in BPHs or flies.' It acknowledges uncertainty about mechanism: 'we cannot exclude additional action of CRZ in neuronal circuits in the CNS' and 'we cannot exclude local CRZ signaling in brain circuits.' The authors note that 'synaptic inputs to CRZ-producing neurons remain to be identified' and that 'the mechanism by which CRZ signaling in the reproductive tract regulates the PMR remains to be determined.' These self-identified limitations form the basis for inferring likely reviewer concerns.

examples.quoted_phrase: Our study unveils a novel function of CRZ in an endogenous female signaling pathway
It is, thus, the first report linking CRZ to a PMR in insects
CRZ may act to regulate the release of stored sperm and seminal fluid in the female
we cannot exclude additional action of CRZ in neuronal circuits in the CNS
demonstrate that CRZ signaling decreases the female responsiveness to courting males

phrase_evidence.phrase: Our study unveils a novel function
It is, thus, the first report linking
our main findings using mutants and RNAi
It is interesting to note that
This can probably be best investigated
suggesting evolutionary conservation of an ancient function
Wild-type males were used in all behavioral assays
Data are shown as mean ± s.e.m.
characterized by reduced receptivity to re-mating and increased oviposition
which ensures numerous and viable offspring

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legend excerpts.
